content.claim: Poland's 2019 teacher strike involving almost 80% of schools was partially motivated by frustration over implementation chaos from the 2016–2017 structural reform, alongside poor working conditions and salaries
content.context: Fresh live research through the Polish system reform lens
limitations: Strike causation was multifactorial; reform-related chaos is one factor among several workplace grievances
Does not quantify the proportion of striker motivation attributable to structural reform versus salary/conditions
Does not measure downstream impact of strike on SEN identification or service delivery
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
